text.title: practices-python-ecosystem Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for practices-python-ecosystem as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Inline DB query inside the route handler body (e.g., `db.query(Item).filter(...).first()` directly in the function).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Untestable without the HTTP layer; the same query appears duplicated in other routes or Celery tasks.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Breaks static analysis, makes agent completions unreliable, and fails `pyright --strict` checks.

failure-mode.description: Using `pip install -r requirements.txt` without a lockfile, or having both `requirements.txt` and `pyproject.toml` with conflicting versions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-locked requirements allow minor version drift that introduces breaking changes silently between installs.
